name: Irish Brown Bread#2 Monica Sheridan
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Stone Ground Whole Wheat Flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the whole wheat flour thoroughly with the white flour.
2. Rub the butter into the flours until well combined.
3. Add the salt and baking soda, mixing until just incorporated.
4. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients and gradually mix in the liquid.
5. Stir with a wooden spoon. You may need less or more liquid depending on the absorbent quality of the flour; the dough should be soft but manageable.
6. Knead the dough into a ball in the mixing bowl using your floured hands.
7. Place the dough on a lightly floured baking sheet and use the palm of your hand to flatten it out into a circle that is about 1 1/2 inches thick.
8. With a knife dipped in flour, make a cross through the center of the bread so it will easily break into quarters when baked.
9. Bake at 425 degrees Fahrenheit for 25 minutes, then reduce the heat to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and bake for an additional 15 minutes.
10. If the crust seems too hard, wrap the baked bread in a damp tea cloth. Leave the loaf standing upright until it is cool.
11. The bread should not be cut until it has set, about 6 hours after it comes out of the oven.
